none.$description: No motion. Used as the reduced-motion target and the default transition delay.

instant.$description: Micro-feedback on small elements — hover, focus rings, toggles, small fades.

fast.$description: Quick transitions over short distances — dropdowns, popovers, exits.

base.$description: Default duration for most UI transitions.

slow.$description: Larger surfaces entering or leaving — modals, drawers, sheets.

slower.$description: Full-screen transitions and background dimming behind overlays.

standard.$description: Standard ease-in-out for elements moving within the viewport, visible from start to end.

decelerate.$description: Entrance (ease-out) for elements appearing in view, fast then settling. Also used for surfaces that leave but stay nearby, ready to return (e.g. a side panel).

accelerate.$description: Exit (ease-in) for elements leaving view permanently, building speed as they go.

linear.$description: No easing. For continuous or progress-driven motion — spinners, progress bars, rotation.

hover.$description: Hover, focus and toggle feedback on interactive elements.

enter.$description: Elements entering the view — menus, popovers, tooltips.

overlay.$description: Modals, drawers and sheets entering over a dimmed background.
